messages.content: Hey everyone! 😄 Random thought—remember those super embarrassing things we did as kids? Anyone wanna share their funniest childhood memory? I could use a good laugh right now!

Omg yes 😂 I have so many! I once tried to run away from home because my mom wouldn’t let me eat ice cream for breakfast. I packed like 2 socks, my favorite stuffed animal, and an apple and made it to the end of the driveway before I got hungry and came back.

Haha Diana that’s adorable! 😂 The classic dramatic exit. I definitely tried to “run away” once too, but I only made it to the backyard with my Ninja Turtles backpack and a packet of fruit snacks. I think I lasted maybe 10 minutes before I got bored and came inside for cartoons.

Lol, love that we all had the same dramatic energy as kids! Did anyone else used to put on “shows” for their family? My cousins and I would force everyone to sit on the couch while we performed these chaotic dance numbers or magic tricks—half the time I’d trip over my own feet or forget the routine, but we thought we were legit stars 😂

Omg yes, we did SO many “shows” for our parents 😂 My brother and I used to make up these totally nonsensical plays and dress up in the weirdest outfits we could find—like, random hats and scarves and even a Halloween costume or two. I remember being super offended once when my mom started laughing in the middle because I thought it was supposed to be serious art 😂

Haha oh man, the living room “shows” bring back memories! My sister and I used to put on these epic lip-sync concerts. We’d grab hairbrushes for microphones and blast our favorite songs, full-on choreography and everything. I swear, our dog was our most loyal (and confused) audience member 😂 One time I got so into it I slipped on the carpet mid-spin and wiped out into a pile of pillows. Total rockstar move.

Haha omg, the hairbrush microphone is such a classic! I definitely did the same thing with a tennis racket—instant rockstar vibes. One time I tried to do a dramatic “stage dive” off the couch but totally misjudged the distance and landed straight on my butt. My parents were like, “Well, that’s enough performing for today!” 😂 Kids are so unintentionally hilarious.

Haha stage diving off the couch is peak childhood energy 😂 I remember trying to do a “magic trick” where I made my little brother disappear—literally just had him duck behind the couch while I waved a blanket dramatically. I was convinced I had real powers for a solid minute until he popped up and started giggling. The audience (my grandma) was VERY impressed, though.

That’s amazing 😂 The sibling magic act! I used to try to convince my little cousin I had psychic powers by “predicting” what color candy he’d pick from a bag—except I always peeked first when he wasn’t looking. He was absolutely convinced for months that I could read his mind. I still feel a tiny bit guilty, but also proud of my very questionable mentalist skills!

Lol Charlie, that’s such a classic big kid move! Honestly, our poor siblings and cousins didn’t stand a chance 😅 Did anyone else have super weird imaginary friends? I had one named Mr. Pineapple who apparently lived in our laundry basket and gave me “life advice” (which was mostly just “eat more cookies”). My parents still tease me about him!

Mr. Pineapple is iconic 😂 I love that his main advice was to eat more cookies—solid life wisdom, honestly! I had an imaginary friend too, but mine was a purple cat named Whiskers who only showed up when I didn’t want to clean my room. I’d totally blame any mess on her and my parents just played along. Looking back, I’m impressed at their patience!

Omg, imaginary friends are the best 😂 I had a whole squad—there was a talking sock named Greg and a fairy who lived in our mailbox. I’d actually write her little notes and stuff them in there, then get mad when the “mail fairy” didn’t write back (aka when my mom forgot to respond). Pretty sure my parents still have those notes somewhere as embarrassing evidence!

Eve, a talking sock named Greg is honestly genius 😂 I love that you even wrote to your mailbox fairy! Why were we all so creative (and a little chaotic) as kids? I used to build “forts” out of couch cushions and convince myself it was a top secret spy base. I’d leave super dramatic notes for my parents with passwords and missions, like “bring me juice or the mission will fail!” I thought I was being so mysterious, but really I just wanted more

Hahaha Diana, the “spy base” demands are too good 😂 I definitely remember making my own “secret headquarters” under the dining table and trying to recruit my parents as agents. My secret codes were just random letters, but I acted like it was serious espionage business. One time I left a note that just said “Operation Snack: commence” and my mom brought me crackers like it was a top secret mission success. 10/10 would do it again tbh.

Bob, “Operation Snack: commence” is honestly a genius move 😂 I feel like snacks were basically the main objective of most of my childhood schemes too. One summer I tried to run a “candy store” out of my bedroom and tried to charge my family for those little mints you get at restaurants—I even made fake money out of construction paper. My mom was my only customer and I’m pretty sure she still has a stash of those paper dollars somewhere!

Alice, I love that you basically started your own candy black market 😂 My brother and I used to “open” a restaurant in the backyard and serve mud pies and grass “salads” to anyone who’d humor us. My poor dad would pretend to eat it and give us reviews like, “Hmm, this dirt has a lovely texture!” Honestly, shoutout to all the parents putting up with our wild childhood businesses and spy ops!

Omg, backyard restaurants and spy ops—our parents deserve medals 😂 I was more of a playground entrepreneur tbh. I’d always hang out by the swing set and try to convince other kids to join my “swing club” (membership perks included taking turns pushing each other and secret handshake access). Honestly, swings were my happy place—I could spend hours just seeing how high I could go or trying to flip all the way around (never succeeded, but dreams were big)! Anyone else obsessed with

Eve, I was definitely part of the “swing club” too, at least in spirit! 🙌 I was obsessed with trying to jump off at the highest point and land like some kind of stuntman. Most of the time it was just awkward landings and grass stains, but I felt so cool for about three seconds in the air 😅 Anyone else remember those super dramatic dares, like “I bet you can’t touch the top bar!” or “Jump over three puddles

Charlie, yes! The classic “bet you can’t…” dares were basically the entire plot of every recess 😂 I remember trying to leap from the swing and land on my feet—usually ended up with a face full of dirt, but it felt like a heroic moment. And
